description: Read data.csv, remove null values, deduplicate rows, cast numeric columns using a predefined mapping, filter rows where salary > 50000, sort by salary descending, compute group-wise count by region, and export the final dataset to etl_output.csv.
Read input.json, store the dataset into a SQLite database called analytics.db in table events, then run a SQL query to retrieve all events, and export the results to events_summary.json.

Read dataset.csv, drop null values, filter rows where score > 0.7, store the filtered data into a SQLite database called predictions.db in a table called results, then expose a REST API endpoint at route /results on port 8080 with API key authentication using environment variable API_SECRET_KEY.
_note: Flask blocks the process so only the database file (written before Flask starts) is checkable. REST criteria verified by compile success + db existence.

description: Read a CSV file named data.csv and perform cleaning, deduplication, filtering where age > 30, sorting by age ascending, and export results to final.csv. The planner may receive this task in mixed phrasing and must still produce a valid DAG pipeline after normalization.
Read a CSV file called data.csv, group by the department column and count rows per group, then export the aggregated result to summary.csv.
